values.brief: The clipping mode of an actor.
values.name: CLIPPING_MODE

values.brief: The color of an actor.
values.name: COLOR

values.brief: The alpha component of an actor's color.
values.name: COLOR_ALPHA

values.brief: The blue component of an actor's color.
values.name: COLOR_BLUE

values.brief: The green component of an actor's color.
values.name: COLOR_GREEN

values.brief: The color mode of an actor.
values.name: COLOR_MODE

values.brief: The red component of an actor's color.
values.name: COLOR_RED

values.brief: The flag whether the actor is connected to the Scene. When an actor is connected, it will be directly or indirectly parented to the root Actor.
values.name: CONNECTED_TO_SCENE

values.brief: Returns whether the actor is culled or not.
values.name: CULLED

values.brief: The draw mode of an actor.
values.name: DRAW_MODE

values.brief: The flag to determine the height dependent on the width.
values.name: HEIGHT_FOR_WIDTH

values.brief: The resize policy for the height of an actor.
values.name: HEIGHT_RESIZE_POLICY

values.brief: The current depth in the hierarchy of the actor.
values.name: HIERARCHY_DEPTH

values.brief: The unique ID of the actor.
values.name: ID

values.brief: Determines whether child actors inherit the layout direction from a parent.
values.name: INHERIT_LAYOUT_DIRECTION

values.brief: The flag whether a child actor inherits it's parent's orientation.
values.name: INHERIT_ORIENTATION

values.brief: The flag whether a child actor inherits it's parent's position.
values.name: INHERIT_POSITION

values.brief: The flag whether a child actor inherits it's parent's scale.
values.name: INHERIT_SCALE

values.brief: The flag whether the actor is of class Dali::Layer.
values.name: IS_LAYER

values.brief: The flag whether an actor is the root actor, which is owned by the Scene.
values.name: IS_ROOT

values.brief: The flag whether the actor should be focusable by keyboard navigation.
values.name: KEYBOARD_FOCUSABLE

values.brief: The direction of the layout.
values.name: LAYOUT_DIRECTION

values.brief: The flag whether an actor should receive a notification when touch or hover motion events leave. In the case of the hover event, when the hover event enters the actor, it will receive started state.
values.name: LEAVE_REQUIRED

values.brief: The maximum size an actor can be assigned in size negotiation.
values.name: MAXIMUM_SIZE

values.brief: The minimum size an actor can be assigned in size negotiation.
values.name: MINIMUM_SIZE

values.brief: The name of an actor.
values.name: NAME

values.brief: The opacity of the actor.
values.name: OPACITY

values.brief: The orientation of an actor.
values.name: ORIENTATION

values.brief: The padding of an actor for use in layout.
values.name: PADDING

values.brief: The origin of an actor, within its parent's area.
values.name: PARENT_ORIGIN

values.brief: The x origin of an actor, within its parent's area.
values.name: PARENT_ORIGIN_X

values.brief: The y origin of an actor, within its parent's area.
values.name: PARENT_ORIGIN_Y

values.brief: The z origin of an actor, within its parent's area.
values.name: PARENT_ORIGIN_Z

values.brief: The anchor-point of an actor.

values.name: PIVOT_X

values.brief: The y anchor-point of an actor.

values.brief: The z anchor-point of an actor.
values.name: PIVOT_Z

values.brief: The position of an actor.

values.name: POSITION_USES_PIVOT

values.brief: The x position of an actor.
values.name: POSITION_X

section_policy.guidance: After the title, overview, and table of contents, choose feature-specific topic sections. Do not use a fixed section template for every feature. Sections should explain meaningful usage themes rather than list APIs mechanically.

rules: Use dali-ui symbols as the app-facing API surface.
Use dali-core and dali-adaptor symbols only as setup/background unless the usage pattern explicitly marks them preferred.
Prefer View-based examples over raw Actor object-model examples.

usage_patterns.intent: Make a View respond to input through the interactive trait.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Obtain the trait from View, then connect signals on the trait. Do not pass trait objects to unrelated effect APIs.

usage_patterns.intent: Animate a View using the dali-ui app-facing animation API.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Use View as the object being animated. Inherited transform property enums are owned by Dali::Actor::Property.

rendered_prompt:
You are generating a DALi dali-ui application developer guide.
Return only the complete Markdown document. Do not include commentary outside Markdown.

Hard requirements:
- Write the final user-facing guide in en only.
- Do not include Korean, Japanese, Chinese, or other non-English prose in the final guide.
- Assume dali-ui is the default application layer.
- Use Dali::Ui::View as the app-facing object model instead of raw Dali::Actor.
- Use only API symbols from the provided Allowed symbols, Property owners, and Usage patterns.
- Explain primary/supporting symbols as owned content when their usage scope says so.
- Use context/reference symbols only for setup, examples, or background lookup.
- Do not invent DALi APIs.
- Do not use raw Actor/SetProperty idioms unless the context pack allows it.
- If you are uncertain about an API, omit that example or use another provided symbol. Do not write unresolved placeholders.
- Do not expose prompt instructions, schema names, uncertainty notes, or validation comments in the user document.
- Start with a feature title, then a very short overview, then a table of contents.
- Create feature-specific topic sections rather than a fixed template.
- Include rich C++ API usage examples and use backticks for API names.
- Prefer concise, compilable-looking snippets over long sample copies.

Source grounding and API verification:
- Write enough practical detail for application developers to use the feature in a dali-ui app.
- Ground natural-language behavior claims in the actual public headers, implementation files, samples, or UTCs when the context pack is not enough.
- Before writing examples, inspect the referenced public headers and relevant sample files when needed.
- Prefer examples copied or adapted from existing samples when they match the feature; otherwise keep examples small and verify every API use.
- After drafting, review the completed Markdown line by line against repository evidence and fix inaccurate explanations or code before returning.
- For every C++ code block, inline API statement, and backticked API name, verify:
  - receiver method exists on the receiver type
  - static methods are called statically and member methods are called on valid instances
  - assignment target is compatible with the method return type
  - argument count, argument type, and overload shape match the public API
  - enum owner and enum value both exist
  - property constants use the correct owner and value type
  - signal callback signatures match the public signal typedef
  - code blocks do not rely on inferred namespaces or overloads from naming alone

Document target:
- Feature: View (Base UI Object) (view)
- Category: views-components
- Guide profile: dali-ui-app
- Audience: application developers using dali-ui public APIs

Structure policy:
{
  "required_shell": [
    "title",
    "overview",
    "table_of_contents"
  ],
  "section_policy": {
    "fixed_sections": false,
    "guidance": "After the title, overview, and table of contents, choose feature-specific topic sections. Do not use a fixed section template for every feature. Sections should explain meaningful usage themes rather than list APIs mechanically.\n",
    "max_topic_sections": 8,
    "min_topic_sections": 2,
    "require_api_examples_per_topic": true,
    "require_backticked_api_names": true
  },
  "markdown": {
    "frontmatter": {
      "enabled": true,
      "fields": {
        "title": "{display_name}",
        "sidebar_label": "{display_name}",
        "category": "{category}"
      }
    },
    "heading_style": "atx",
    "code_fence_language": "cpp"
  },
  "content_quality": {
    "overview_style": "very_short",
    "require_table_of_contents": true,
    "require_rich_api_examples": true,
    "require_backticks_for_api_names": true,
    "topic_sections_must_be_feature_specific": true,
    "avoid_mechanical_api_listing": true,
    "default_object_model": "Use Dali::Ui::View rather than Dali::Actor for app-facing object examples.",
    "guide_assumption": "The reader is using dali-ui to build applications."
  }
}

Surface and usage rules:
{
  "primary_surface": "dali-ui",
  "rules": [
    "Use dali-ui symbols as the app-facing API surface.",
    "Use dali-core and dali-adaptor symbols only as setup/background unless the usage pattern explicitly marks them preferred.",
    "Prefer View-based examples over raw Actor object-model examples."
  ],
  "symbol_counts": {
    "dali-ui": 180
  }
}

Constraints:
{
  "avoid_idioms_unless_needed": [
    "raw_actor_set_property"

 * - Blue box at bottom-right area (280, 400)
 *
 * Press Escape or Back to quit.
 */

{
public:
  AbsoluteLayoutController(Application& application)
    : mApplication(application)
  {
    mApplication.InitSignal().Connect(this, &AbsoluteLayoutController::Create);
  }

  void Create(Application application)
  {
    Window window = application.GetWindow();
    window.SetBackgroundColor(Color::WHITE);

* Same content as absolutelayout-example. A 100x50 yellow standalone
 * child at the top-left toggles the root view's LayoutDirection between
 * LEFT_TO_RIGHT and RIGHT_TO_LEFT on click. RTL flips direct children
 * horizontally; the standalone toggle button is excluded from mirroring
 * and stays at the top-left.
 *
 * Press Escape or Back to quit.
 */
class AbsoluteLayoutDirectionController : public ConnectionTracker
{
public:
  AbsoluteLayoutDirectionController(Application& application)
    : mApplication(application)
